excerpts.text: The Chinese possess the light of reason; they have the most ancient of all liberal traditions; and their books are not without a certain wisdom.
I have made myself Chinese in order to win the Chinese for Christ.
excerpts.source_title: Letter to Claudio Acquaviva, Superior General

excerpts.text: The Doctrine of the Lord of Heaven does not destroy the teachings of Confucius but rather completes them.
excerpts.source_title: The True Meaning of the Lord of Heaven (Tianzhu Shiyi)

excerpts.text: I have labored for twenty years in this vineyard, and now I see the first fruits beginning to ripen.
Among all the pagan nations known to Europe, I know of no one which erred less in matters of faith than the Chinese in the early ages of their history.
Friendship is the most pleasant thing in the world; without a friend even the most agreeable pursuits become tedious.
A friend is another self; thus one who has a friend may say that he is two, and one who has no friend may say that he is but half a man.
Friendship is of four kinds: that which arises from virtue, that from profit, that from pleasure, and that from habit; the first alone is true and lasting.
The whole Chinese government resembles a well-ordered family. The emperor is father, and his subjects are his children.
In this kingdom there are three sects: the literati, the worshippers of idols, and the disciples of Laozi. The literati hold the first place.
I am sending Your Reverence a map of the world which I have translated into Chinese. It has aroused great interest, and many copies have been made.
We have adopted the garments and customs of the literati, the most honored class in the kingdom, and we live as they do.
The Chinese have a natural goodness which has been admirably described by their philosophers, and their ancient books contain maxims worthy of Christianity.
Whoever wishes to be useful to the Chinese must first become one of them in dress, in manners, and in life.
I have translated the Four Books of the Chinese into Latin, that Europe may know what moral philosophy these people have attained by the light of nature alone.
The Chinese are so attached to their own customs that they think the rest of the world is blind and that they alone can see.
God has opened the door of China to me, and I do not intend to close it. I shall continue to knock until I am admitted to the very court of the Son of Heaven.
The ancient Chinese knew and worshipped the one true God, whom they called the Lord of Heaven. It is the later corruption of Buddhism that introduced idolatry.
excerpts.source_title: The True Meaning of the Lord of Heaven (Tianzhu Shiyi)

excerpts.text: By the clocks, the mathematical instruments, and the maps, I have won the respect of the mandarins, and through that respect, access to speak of the things of God.
Today I was received by the Emperor, who accepted the gifts I brought — clocks, a clavichord, paintings, and books. Now we have his protection.
I am now at the end of my strength. The labors of twenty-seven years in this mission have consumed me. But I leave behind a door open to great things.
What the ancient Chinese called Shangdi, the Lord on High, and what they called Tian, Heaven — this is none other than the God we preach.
excerpts.source_title: The True Meaning of the Lord of Heaven (Tianzhu Shiyi)

excerpts.text: The art of memory is held in great wonder here. I showed them how to place images in rooms of the mind, and they believe it is the art of the Western sages.
If one adapts the ceremonies of ancestor worship, stripping them of superstition, they may serve as acts of filial piety compatible with the Christian faith.
Paul Xu Guangqi is the greatest of all our converts — a man of deep learning and deep faith, who may yet transform this empire.
